title: Today's AI Brief: Gemini 3.5 and AI in Admin
summary: Google's new Gemini 3.5 model and AI in admin departments
imageAlt: Image from source article for Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action

body.text: I'm checking out some fascinating developments in artificial intelligence. Google just unveiled its latest model, Gemini 3.5, which promises to bring frontier intelligence with action. This model is built to help execute complex workflows, which could have significant implications for various industries. I'm intrigued to see how this will play out.

According to Google, Gemini 3.5 is designed to handle agentic workflows, which means it can perform tasks that typically require human-like intelligence and action. This could be a game-changer for areas like automation and data analysis. The potential for increased efficiency and productivity is substantial. I'm curious to see how this model will be adopted and what kind of impact it will have on businesses and individuals alike.

In another development, AI is starting to make its way into admin departments, particularly in small businesses. From accounting to design to market research, AI is being leveraged to streamline processes and improve efficiency. It's exciting to think about the possibilities - with AI handling routine tasks, human resources can focus on more strategic and creative work. This could be a significant productivity booster for many companies.

Gemini 3.5: frontier intelligence with action is also worth a quick mention.
